title: Pen Pens Nature
wikitext:
[]: Pen-Pen's Nature             Quote Post by Storm » Tue Jan 16, 2018 10:34 pm Upon my most recent NGE re-watch, I'm forced to conclude a fundamental truth about Pen-Pen's nature: Namely, that those who have seen human death are unable to see or interact with Pen-Pen. Asuka never seems to acknowledge Pen-Pen at all - or even to notice him. After Shinji kills Kaworu, he's never seen to interact with Pen-Pen again - but sees him during Instrumentality. Same for after Misato kills the JSSDF personnel; she clearly remembers the existence of Pen-Pen, but we never see them together again, due to Misato's subsequent death and the end of the world. Akira died moments after Misato's rescue pod sealed, so she can't be said to have seen him die. This is all clearly leading up to a larger point: we never see someone who has been confirmed to see human death interact with that lovable hot-spring penguin.

[]: Re: Pen-Pen's Nature             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jan 17, 2018 7:11 am Shinji was eye witness to his mother being absorbed by Unit 01, and thought she died. Also, Misato was at Ground Zero for Second Impact, where her father and everyone else died horribly.

[]: Re: Pen-Pen's Nature             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Wed Jan 17, 2018 11:07 am AuraTwilight wrote: Shinji was eye witness to his mother being absorbed by Unit 01, and thought she died.

[]: Re: Pen-Pen's Nature             Quote Post by Bryan » Fri Jan 19, 2018 12:57 am AdamMalkobitch wrote: Was it ever stated in Neon Genesis that he had forgotten he was a witness? I know in New Theatrical, during the shogi scene, Fuyutsuki mentions that Shinji's "memories have since been erased"

[]: Re: Pen-Pen's Nature             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Fri Jan 19, 2018 11:02 am Bryan wrote: No way, that's some special bullshit concocted for the New Theatrical. Or not, we'll see where they go with it.

[]: Re: Pen-Pen's Nature             Quote Post by mys_721tx » Tue Mar 20, 2018 7:31 pm Storm wrote: Upon my most recent NGE re-watch, I'm forced to conclude a fundamental truth about Pen-Pen's nature: Namely, that those who have seen human death are unable to see or interact with Pen-Pen. Asuka never seems to acknowledge Pen-Pen at all - or even to notice him. After Shinji kills Kaworu, he's never seen to interact with Pen-Pen again - but sees him during Instrumentality. Same for after Misato kills the JSSDF personnel; she clearly remembers the existence of Pen-Pen, but we never see them together again, due to Misato's subsequent death and the end of the world. Akira died moments after Misato's rescue pod sealed, so she can't be said to have seen him die. This is all clearly leading up to a larger point: we never see someone who has been confirmed to see human death interact with that lovable hot-spring penguin.

[]: Re: Pen-Pen's Nature             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Apr 07, 2018 12:18 pm mys_721tx wrote: After Unit 00's self-destruction (Ep. 23), Misato deemed Tyoko-III to be too dangerous for Pen-pen to live and sent him to stary with Hikari's family. Kaworu showed up an episode after that.

Same for after Misato kills the JSSDF personnel; she clearly remembers the existence of Pen-Pen, but we never see them together again, due to Misato's subsequent death and the end of the world. Akira died moments after Misato's rescue pod sealed, so she can't be said to have seen him die. This is all clearly leading up to a larger point: we never see someone who has been confirmed to see human death interact with that lovable hot-spring penguin.
